description_long: This object is a modern, compact airplane with a streamlined fuselage and sharply swept-back wings. The wings are mid-mounted and feature a slight anhedral angle, with no visible winglets. The tail section is equipped with a conventional horizontal stabilizer and a single vertical stabilizer, forming a standard tail configuration. The cockpit is positioned at the front of the fuselage and is enclosed with a clear canopy, suggesting a single or dual-seat arrangement. The engine is mounted at the rear of the fuselage, with a prominent exhaust nozzle visible, indicating a jet propulsion system. The landing gear appears to be retractable, with the main gear housed under the wings and a nose gear under the forward fuselage.
description: A sleek, modern jet airplane with swept-back mid-mounted wings, a single rear jet engine, and a forward enclosed cockpit. The tail features a conventional stabilizer and a single vertical fin.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the swept-back wings, central fuselage, and the full span of the horizontal stabilizer.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the streamlined fuselage, cockpit canopy, and the rear-mounted jet engine.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the engine exhaust, tail assembly, and the anhedral angle of the wings.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the landing gear doors and the overall aerodynamic profile.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with sharply swept-back wings and a slight anhedral angle, no winglets or variable geometry features.
attribute_engine_type: Single rear-mounted jet engine with a prominent exhaust nozzle.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers forming a standard tailplane.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear with main gear under the wings and a single nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a clear canopy, likely single or dual-seat configuration.
